4 Quality Information Document for 
OMI_VAR_EXTREME_CLIMVAR_PACIFIC_npgo_sla_eof_mode_projection 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-OMI-QUID-VAR_EXTREME-
CLIMVAR-NPGO 
Issue 4.0 –November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_VAR_EXTREME_CLIMVAR_PACIFIC_npgo_sla_eof_mode_projection. 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
 
The North Pacific Gyre Oscillation (NPGO) is a climate pattern introduced by Di 
Lorenzo et al. (2008). The NPGO is defined as the second dominant mode of variability 
of Sea Surface Height (SSH) anomaly in Northeast Pacific (25°–62°N, 180°–250°E, see 
Figure 2 of Di Lorenzo et al. (2008). From the principal component analysis, the EOF’s 
amplitude pattern or correlation map has been inferred and projected on the SSH 
anomalies from an historical eddy-permitting model hindcast of the Northeast Pacific 
over the period 1950-2004. 
From 2004 onward, the CMEMS NPGO index OMI is derived from the last version (DT-
2024, see https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/QUID/CMEMS-SL-QUID-
008-032-068.pdf) of the reprocessed altimeter sea level delayed-time record 
(SEALEVEL_GLO_PHY_L4_MY_008_047) and from the near real time altimeter sea level 
(SEALEVEL_GLO_PHY_L4_NRT _008_046).  
Sea level change time series have a strong seasonal signal and long-term trend. The 
altimeter data record is used from year 1993 to fit a model on annual, semi-annual sea 
level variability, and linear trend changes. This model is then used to remove these 
signals on each grid points of the altimeter map series. The temporal variations 
corresponding to the NPGO EOF mode are deduced from the series of sea level grid 
obtained. The NPGO index obtained is centered and normalized to fit with the Di 
Lorenzo index (Di Lorenzo et al., 2008), over the [1993, 2003] period. Finally, NPGO 
index deduced from altimetry in completed with historical index from Di Lorenzo et al 
(2008) from 2004 downward.  
 
A detailed description of the North Pacific Gyre Oscillation index has been provided 
by Tranchant et al. (2019) in section 2.6 of the CMEMS Ocean State Report #3 and this 
section is included in Erreur ! Source du renvoi introuvable. of this document.
